title: Manages document update history logging and replay with atomic file operations
purpose: Tracks document changes over time with timestamps and diffs, enabling history retrieval and content reconstruction at any point in time. Uses atomic file operations to ensure data consistency.
module_context: Exports a UpdateLogManager class that handles logging and replaying document updates with support for multiple resource types including documents, diagrams, designs, spreadsheets, and snippets.

steps.content: Initialize the base path for the session folder.
Set the log file path to update-log.json within the session folder.

methods.name: logUpdate
methods.normalized_params: (resourceType: ResourceType, resourceId: string, oldContent: string, newContent: string, diff?: { oldString: string; newString: string }): Promise<void>

steps.content: Skip logging if the old and new content are identical.
Load the existing update log from disk.
Create a new log entry for the resource if this is the first update.
Compute a diff if one is not provided.
Create a change entry with the current timestamp and diff.
Append the change entry to the resource's change history.
Save the updated log to disk atomically.
Load the update log from disk.
Return the history for the specified resource or null if not found.

methods.name: replayToTimestamp
methods.normalized_params: (resourceType: ResourceType, resourceId: string, timestamp: string): Promise<string>

steps.content: Retrieve the history for the specified resource.
Throw an error if no history is found for the resource.
Parse the target timestamp into a numeric time value.
Initialize content with the original resource content.
Iterate through changes in chronological order up to the target timestamp.
Apply each change by replacing the old string with the new string in the content.
Stop applying changes once a timestamp exceeds the target.
Return the reconstructed content at the target timestamp.
Check if the log file exists at the configured path.
Return an empty log with all resource type keys if the file does not exist.
Read the content of the log file from disk.
Parse the JSON content into a log object.
Ensure all resource type keys exist in the log for backwards compatibility.
Return the parsed log object.
IF file reading or JSON parsing fails, return an empty log and log a warning.
Create a temporary file path for the log.
Serialize the log object into formatted JSON text.
Write the JSON content to the temporary file.
Atomically rename the temporary file to the actual log file path.
IF the rename operation fails, delete the temporary file and re-throw the error.
IF the write operation fails, throw an error indicating the save failure.
